# breeze daemon's own lifecycle surface: --help must never silently daemonize,
# ping/status must show the resolved dir, and daemon restart must actually work.
env BREEZE_DIR=$WORK/state

# A regression test in itself: this used to silently fall through to starting a
# real blocking foreground daemon instead of printing usage.
! exec breeze daemon --help
stderr 'not a recognized flag'
! exists $WORK/state/breeze.sock

# Auto-start on first routine use.
exec breeze ping
stdout 'pong'
stdout 'dir \S+/state'

exec breeze status --json
stdout '"dir":\s*"\S+/state"'

# Explicit restart: nothing to displace on the very first explicit invocation of
# this kind is fine — restartDaemon falls back to a fresh detached start.
exec breeze daemon restart
stdout 'restart'

exec breeze ping
stdout 'pong'

exec breeze stop
